title: Quiet: The Power of Introverts in a World That Can't Stop Talking
subtitle: A groundbreaking exploration of introversion and the hidden strengths of quiet, thoughtful people in an extrovert-centric culture.
author: Susan Cain
themes: The biological basis of introversion and extraversion
The cultural bias toward the Extrovert Ideal
The hidden strengths of quiet leadership
The power of soft power in negotiations and relationships
How introverts and extroverts can communicate and collaborate effectively
awards: New York Times Bestseller (over 2 years on list)
TIME Magazine Top 10 Nonfiction Books of 2012
excerpt: At least one-third of the people you know are introverts. In a world that prizes the loud, the outgoing, and the gregarious, introverts are often undervalued and overlooked. Susan Cain makes a powerful case for the quiet revolutionaries — the thinkers, creators, and leaders whose introversion is not a weakness but a superpower. Drawing on neuroscience, evolutionary psychology, and her own experience, Cain shows how introverts can thrive in an extrovert-centric world.
